name: know-tool
displayName: Know Tool - Product-Code Alignment for LLMs
description: Master the know CLI tool for bridging product requirements and code. Use when working with spec-graph.json to map user objectives to software components, eliminate spec brittleness, and provide LLMs with consistent product-code understanding.
longDescription: An LLM-first approach to product-driven software development that replaces brittle spec.md files with a single source of truth. Maps user objectives directly to software components, eliminating hallucination and inconsistency. The graph structure connects WHAT users want with HOW the system implements it, giving LLMs token-efficient access to complete product-code understanding without repetitive manual analysis.
features: Map user objectives to software components in a single source of truth
Eliminate spec.md brittleness and LLM hallucination
Query product-code relationships efficiently without token waste
Track what users want (WHAT) and how systems deliver it (HOW)
Validate product completeness with gap analysis
Access detailed guides for product-driven development patterns
useCases: Replacing brittle spec.md files with single source of truth
Mapping user objectives to software components for LLMs
Eliminating hallucination by providing consistent product-code context
Querying what users want and how the system delivers it
Validating that all features trace back to user objectives
Maintaining product-code alignment as requirements evolve
Planning implementations with full user-intent traceability

productCodeAlignment.problem: spec.md files are brittle, prone to hallucination, resistant to change, and inconsistent
productCodeAlignment.solution: Single source of truth mapping user intent to implementation
productCodeAlignment.benefit: LLMs get token-efficient, consistent product-code understanding without repetitive analysis

dualGraphSystem.specGraph: Maps user intent: user → objective → feature → component (WHAT users want)
dualGraphSystem.codeGraph: Maps implementation: module → package → external-dep (HOW it's built)
dualGraphSystem.integration: product-component references link code modules to spec components
dualGraphSystem.discovery: Use 'know -g <graph-path> rules graph' to see valid relationships for each graph type

twoChains.what: project → user → objective → action (what users want to accomplish)
twoChains.how: project → requirement → interface → feature → action → component → operation (how system delivers it)
twoChains.bridge: Actions connect user intent with system implementation

structure.entities: Product and code concepts that form the dependency map
structure.references: Detailed specs (business logic, data models, UI patterns, etc.)
structure.meta: Project context (phases, assumptions, decisions, Q&A sessions)

criticalRules: Use 'know -g <graph-path> rules graph' to see valid entity relationships for each graph type
Different graphs have different rules: spec-graph (user→objective→feature→component) vs code-graph (module→package→external-dep)
Use 'know trace' to traverse the product-code boundary and see full upstream/downstream chains
Always validate after modifications - ensures product-code consistency
Map user objectives to actual components - close the intent-to-implementation gap
Run 'know -g <graph> rules describe <entity-type>' to understand what an entity type can depend on
Run gap-analysis regularly - catches incomplete product specifications
Keep relationships explicit in graph section - no implied dependencies
Reference detailed specs without duplication - single source of truth principle
Validate completeness with 'know check link gap-analysis' - ensure features trace to user needs

referenceGuides.description: Deep dive on all entity types and their roles
Understanding reference categories and when to use them
Common patterns: adding features, connecting actions, validation

workflow: Understand how to map your concept: know rules describe feature
Add the product requirement: know add feature new-feature '{"name":"...", "description":"..."}'
Check what implementation pieces it needs: know rules after feature
Connect to user objectives and components: know link feature:new-feature action:user-action
Validate product-code alignment: know check validate && know check link gap-analysis feature:new-feature
quickStart.philosophy: Start with user objectives, map to features, connect to components. The graph enforces traceability.

installation.note: Requires know CLI to be installed globally. Install with: npm install -g know-cli
